Definition: A uniquely identified area of land managed by the company, characterized by name, location, type, status, boundaries (as applicable), and related documentation.
A land parcel identified for exploration that may later be converted into a claim, mineral lease, or private land parcel depending on ownership and mineral findings.

A parcel of public land for which exclusive mineral rights have been filed and approved by the managing agency; claims have corners/boundaries specified for filing and may be active, inactive, or abandoned.
A parcel of private land where mineral rights have been negotiated and leased to the company, with defined start and expiration dates.
A land parcel owned outright by the company; may include surface ownership alone or both surface and mineral rights and can be used for mining operations, equipment storage, or other activities.
A governance/operational state of a land parcel. Claims can be active, inactive, or abandoned; prospects, mineral leases, and private land parcels may be active or inactive.
A corner point defining a claim boundary as declared in claim filing; corners are specified using geographic coordinates and may reference natural landmarks as notes.
A geographic point used to define the outline of a parcel boundary and to store mapping and legal description notes.
The approximate size of a land parcel in acres, calculated using GPS corner coordinates and stored for reference.

The process of converting all or part of a prospect into a claim, mineral lease, or private land parcel once sufficient minerals are confirmed; the original prospect and its details must be retained for history.
An individual or organization associated with mining operations (e.g., partners, contractors, workers, laboratories, refineries, surveyors), with contact details and notes.

A role that a party plays with respect to a land parcel (e.g., prospector, partner, worker, stakeholder), used to manage permissions, responsibilities, and business relationships tied to the property.
A party that performs assay analysis on ore samples and returns assay reports used for decision-making (e.g., whether to file a claim or continue working a property).

A party that receives extracted material payloads and separates/purifies minerals, returning refined minerals while retaining a predetermined fee (often a percentage by weight) and possibly selling minerals on the market as agreed.
A party responsible for collecting ore samples; recorded to support follow-up and traceability regarding sample collection.
A broad category of mining based on the nature of deposits. In this case study: placer mining and hard-rock mining.
Source: Gold Prospecting Narrative (Types of Mining and Methods Used section)

Definition: Extraction of valuable minerals from loose, unconsolidated alluvial deposits found in streambeds, riverbeds, and ancient sediment layers.
Source: Gold Prospecting Narrative (Types of Mining and Methods Used section)

Definition: Extraction of minerals directly from solid rock formations by breaking, blasting, and processing parent rock to release embedded minerals.
Source: Gold Prospecting Narrative (Types of Mining and Methods Used section)

Definition: A specific technique used within a mining type to extract minerals (e.g., panning, sluicing, high-banking, dry-washing, suction dredging; drilling, blasting, cut-and-fill, block caving).
The legal restriction status of a mining method by jurisdiction, typically categorized as legal, restricted, or banned.
A physical sample of ore deposit material (often dirt, gravel, sand) collected from a land parcel before mining; samples may be sent to laboratories for assay analysis.
Source: Gold Prospecting Narrative (About the Minerals and Ore Samples section)

Definition: A laboratory analysis of an ore sample to determine the presence and relative quantities of certain minerals; results are returned in an assay report.
Source: Gold Prospecting Narrative (Sufficient Expected Minerals? section)

Definition: A request sent to a laboratory to perform an assay on an ore sample; may be repeated for comparative reports and includes associated requests and returned reports.
Source: Gold Prospecting Narrative (Documents section under Ore Samples)

Definition: A document produced by a laboratory in response to an assay order, used to support decisions such as filing a claim or continuing work on a property.
Source: Gold Prospecting Narrative (Sufficient Expected Minerals? section)

Definition: A record of whether minerals expected/found on a property are present in sufficient quantity and accessibility to warrant mining efforts, based on assay results.
Source: Gold Prospecting Narrative (Sufficient Expected Minerals? section)

Definition: A naturally occurring substance of value tracked by the company; gold is the primary mineral of interest, with other minerals (e.g., silver, copper, platinum, uranium) possibly present.
Source: Gold Prospecting Narrative (About the Minerals and Ore Samples section)

Definition: A mineral requiring special precautions and typically avoided by the company (example given: uranium).
Source: Gold Prospecting Narrative (About the Minerals and Ore Samples section)

Definition: A record of extracted material shipped from a land parcel, capturing logistics such as container type/identifier, weights, moisture, shipment status, approvals, and refinery receiving details; may be linked to an ore sample for comparison.
An approval action by a party associated with a site material payload for shipment, supporting transportation and accountability.
The process where extracted material is sent to a refinery to separate and purify minerals; outputs include refined minerals and refinery reports/invoices.
Source: Gold Prospecting Narrative (Refinement and Documents sections)

Definition: A purified mineral output returned from the refinery to the company, after the refiner retains a predetermined fee (often by weight).
Operational activities capturing extraction, shipment, refining, and tracking of refined quantities per mineral per land parcel over time (e.g., monthly and year-to-date totals).
Payments to parties based on mineral production, typically calculated as a percentage of minerals extracted/refined from a specific parcel during a period, valued using spot price at time of payment.
Source: Gold Prospecting Narrative (Production Compensation Arrangements section)

Definition: Contractual terms specifying a party’s compensation rate and effective dates for a role tied to a specific parcel and mineral extraction arrangement; must be versioned over time as rates change.
Source: Gold Prospecting Narrative (Documents section under Production Compensation)

Definition: The current market price of a mineral used to compute the dollar value of production compensation at the time of payment.
Mining equipment, machinery, and tools located at or assigned to specific properties, tracked for inventory, maintenance, utilization, and availability even when not actively used in production.

A supporting artifact (typically PDF and/or electronic data) linked to business objects (e.g., parcels, parties, assay orders, payloads) such as contracts, permits, photographs, invoices, reports, and correspondence.
Source: Gold Prospecting Narrative (Documents sections across domains)
